name: Nupe
notes: Nupe (Nupe-Nupe-Tako), a Nupoid (Volta-Niger, Atlantic-Congo) language of Niger and Kwara States, central Nigeria. ORTHOGRAPHY: Latin-based, 24 base letters (a b c d e f g h i j k l m n o p r s t u v w y z) plus the digraphs dz, gb, kp, sh, ts and zh. Letter values that differ from a naive IPA reading: ⟨c⟩ = /t͡ʃ/, ⟨j⟩ = /d͡ʒ/, ⟨y⟩ = /j/, ⟨sh⟩ = /ʃ/, ⟨zh⟩ = /ʒ/, ⟨ts⟩ = /t͡s/, ⟨dz⟩ = /d͡z/, ⟨gb⟩ = /ɡ͡b/, ⟨kp⟩ = /k͡p/; ⟨r⟩ is a trill/tap. Vowels: oral /i e a o u/ with nasal counterparts /ĩ ã ũ/. LABIALISATION AND PALATALISATION: Nupe has extensive secondary articulation, written consonant + ⟨w⟩ and consonant + ⟨y⟩. These are NOT enumerated as separate grapheme keys: the sources consulted do not fix a closed list of Cw / Cy digraphs, so ⟨w⟩ and ⟨y⟩ are transcribed as the glides /w/ and /j/, which under-specifies Cʷ/Cʲ. NASAL VOWELS: written vowel + ⟨n⟩ (an = /ã/, in = /ĩ/, un = /ũ/), homographic with a vowel followed by consonantal /n/; without lexical or syllable-structure context the mapping cannot disambiguate, so ⟨n⟩ always yields /n/ and nasal vowels are under-generated. INPUT CONTRACT: input is assumed to be the modern Latin (NOT Ajami) Nupe orthography, one word per token, already normalised. TONE: Nupe is tonal — three level tones plus falling and rising contours. Tone is marked with diacritics in dictionaries and careful writing (´ high, ` low, unmarked mid, ˆ/ˇ contours) but is routinely omitted in everyday text. This spec transcribes SEGMENTS ONLY: tone diacritics are not converted into tone marks, and where tone is unwritten it is NOT RECOVERABLE from the orthography.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Nupe language
sources.notes: Consonant and vowel charts annotated with the orthographic grapheme for each phoneme (kp, gb, ts, dz, c, j, sh, zh, y, r), plus the tone-marking table; basis of the grapheme map here.

sources.author: Wiktionary contributors
sources.title: Wiktionary:Nupe entry guidelines
sources.notes: Orthography conventions: digraphs dz, gb, kp, sh, ts, zh; nasal vowels written V + n; tone marked acute (high) / grave (low) / unmarked (mid).

sources.author: Blench, Roger M.
sources.title: Oral literature genres of the Nupe of Central Nigeria
sources.notes: Documents the Latin and Ajami writing traditions of Nupe (cited by the Wikipedia article for the script statement).

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
